---
title: "Picrust"
author: "SD"
date: "2026-03-23"
output: html_document
---

```{r library, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(phyloseq)
library(ggpicrust2)
library(latex2exp)
```
# Picrust Analysis of MetaCyc
This pipeline will be a complex analysis of the Picrust output we did on the class server.

## Input and Convert the Needed Files

Input and convert the ```phyloseq_taxonomy.rds``` sample data into a data frame and move the ```fastq``` sample IDs into a column. Then import the ```path_abun_unstrat.tsv``` file.

```{r Import Data}
meta = readRDS('../datasets/ps_filtered.rds') %>% .@sam_data %>% data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column('sample_name')

metacyc = read_delim('../datasets/picrust_output/pathways_out/path_abun_unstrat.tsv')
```

```{r Group}
fast = c('accelerator','crusier sprinter','manoeuvrer')
meta = meta %>%
  mutate(swim_group = ifelse(swim_performance %in% fast,
                      'Fast',
                      ifelse(swim_performance == 'generalist',
                      'Moderate',
                      'Slow'
                      )
  ),
  .after = swim_performance
  )

# For compatibility reasons, fast v slow

meta_fast_slow = meta %>%
  filter(
    swim_group == c('Fast','Slow')
  )
```


## Run the Pipeline

```{r DAA}

# 1. Subset metadata to hindgut only
hindgut <- meta_fast_slow %>% filter(sample_type == "hindgut")

# 2. Keep only hindgut samples that actually exist in MetaCyc
valid_samples <- intersect(hindgut$sample_name, colnames(metacyc))


# 3. Filter MetaCyc to those samples
metacyc_filt <- metacyc %>% 
  select(pathway,all_of(valid_samples)) %>% 
  column_to_rownames("pathway")

# Perform pathway differential abundance analysis (DAA) using LinDA method
daa_results_df = pathway_daa(abundance = metacyc_filt,
                             metadata = hindgut, 
                             group = "swim_group", 
                             daa_method = "LinDA", 
                             select = NULL, reference = NULL)

# saveRDS(daa_results_df, '../datasets/meta_fish_daa_results_df.rds')
```

## Convert to MetaCyc

```{r}
# Annotate pathway results using MetaCyc

daa_annotated_results_df = pathway_annotation(pathway = "MetaCyc",
                                              daa_results_df = daa_results_df,
                                              ko_to_kegg = FALSE)

saveRDS(daa_annotated_results_df,'../datasets/fish_daa_meta_pathway_annotation_results_df.rds')

# daa_annotated_results_df<-readRDS('../datasets/fish_daa_meta_pathway_annotation_results_df.rds')
```

```{r Box Plot}

counts = apply(
    metacyc_filt,
    2,
    function(x) {
      x/sum(x)
    }
) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column(var = 'pathway')

box = counts %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = `13414.101.hg.R1.fastq.gz`:`13414.98.hg.R1.fastq.gz`,
    names_to = 'sample_name',
    values_to = 'count'
  ) %>% 
  left_join(hindgut)

path_interest = box %>% 
  filter(pathway == c("PWY-4702","PWY-7579"))

boxplot = ggplot(path_interest, aes(x = swim_group, y = count, fill = swim_group))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  geom_jitter(color = 'black', size = 3,alpha = 0.6)+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c('#8E7CA6',"#fcba65"))+
  facet_wrap(~ pathway)+
  labs(x='Swim Speed', y='Count')
boxplot

ggsave('../plots/pathway_box.jpg',
       plot = boxplot,
       height=8.5, width = 14)
```


## View Sample Similarity

```{r Volcano Plot}
# This code is optimized for the slow fast comparison

volcano_meta = ggplot(data = daa_annotated_results_df, aes(x=log2FoldChange, y = -log10(p_adjust)))+
  geom_point(colour = '#8E7CA6')+
  aes(alpha = 0.25)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed', color = '#E07A9A')+
  ylim(NA,2.5)+
  theme(legend.position='none')+
  labs(x=TeX('$Log_2$ Fold Change'), y=TeX('$-Log_{10}$ P-Adjusted Value'))
volcano_meta

volcano_meta_labelled = volcano_meta+
    geom_text(
    aes(
      label = ifelse(
        -log10(p_adjust)>-log10(0.05),
        as.character(description),
        ''
      )
    ),
    angle = 45,
    hjust = -0.05,
    vjust = -0.5
  )
volcano_meta_labelled

ggsave('../plots/meta_volcano_labelled.jpg',
       plot = volcano_meta_labelled,
       height=8.5, width = 14)

# log2fold is Change in abundance
# Slow/fast
# more abundant slow in + log2 changes
# More abundant fast in - log2change
# No need for log2fold cut off, enough change as is, does not affect figure interpretation.
```


Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
